title: Increased air pollution exposure among the Chinese population during the national quarantine in 2020
methods: ensemble deep-learning data fusion (CMAQ + CNEMC observations)
dynamic migration modelling (census + Baidu mobility data)
data_sources: CNEMC National Urban Air Quality Real-time Platform
National household energy survey (Shen et al. 2019) + China Statistical Yearbook

data_scope.migration_baseline: 138 million migrant workers (census 2010), scaled to 125 million in 2020

core_conclusion: Despite a 16.6 ug/m3 decrease in ambient (outdoor) PM2.5 during China's COVID-19 national quarantine, total population-weighted PM2.5 exposure increased by 5.7 ug/m3 (95% CI: 1.2-11.0). The increase was driven by urban-to-rural migration frozen by the quarantine, trapping more people in rural households dependent on solid fuels. The quarantine deepened health inequality: rural and lower-income populations bore disproportionate exposure increases while urban populations saw decreases.
why_it_matters: Overturns the 'clean-air dividend' narrative of lockdowns; integrates ambient, indoor, and mobility data into one PWE framework; reveals solid-fuel use as the dominant inequality amplifier; provides the first quantitative evaluation of China's Clean Heating Plan under a natural experiment; findings are generalisable to any society with large indoor pollution burdens.
why_nature: Behaviour-environment coupling at national scale; counter-intuitive high-impact finding overturning prevalent assumptions; environmental equity links to social inequality central to NHB scope; timely and policy-translatable during the pandemic; open data (OSF) and open-source model code align with reproducibility standards.
added_value_to_my_research: Mobile-phone trajectory data replaces aggregate Baidu flows -> individual-level exposure tracking analogous to 2SFCA travel-time weighting
Income-PWE regression -> empirical benchmark for equity-weighted facility-siting optimization (Clean Heating Plan as a spatial resource-allocation problem)
SHAP-attributed ML on county-level PWE change -> explainable GeoAI health-impact prediction without requiring full chemical-transport model runs
Decomposition framework extended to urbanization trajectory -> MGWR detection of non-stationary income-exposure relationships over time
Coupling indoor PM2.5 model with heat-stress layer -> compound multi-hazard environmental health index across urban-rural gradient

figures.caption: Daily trends of PWE in the real case and counterfactual scenarios (2019 migration; no migration) during the study period, showing PWE rose during quarantine despite falling ambient concentrations
Decomposition of PWE change between P1 and P2 into contributions from ambient air quality, population relocation, household energy consumption, and time spent indoors
Daily migration intensity and rural-population fraction around Spring Festivals 2019 and 2020, showing quarantine froze the returning-to-work migration at 18% completion
Quarantine-induced PWE changes and solid-fuel shares across China, by indoor/outdoor, urban/rural, heating/non-heating regions, and income quintiles
County-level spatial distribution of quarantine-induced PWE change across mainland China, with the Qin-Huai Line separating heating from non-heating regions
